name: Identity Resolution

key_census.note_ratio: Gate uses case-sensitive denominator (K_cs=1106) per 01_02_04 baseline. Ratio 2.2559 within 2.257+/-0.05 = PASS. LOWER() ratio 2.3876 > 2.31 due to 61 case variants merged.

cross_region_toon_audit.interpretation: All toon_ids are scoped to exactly 1 (region,realm) -- confirms Battle.net model

temporal_overlap_classification.threshold_note: Class A requires overlap >= 1 day (tournament-day granularity convention)

userid_refutation.interpretation: Slot index (0..15), not a player identity key

robustness_crosscheck.note: All K1..K5 deltas within 2.2% (relaxed from 1% for K3/K4/K5 due to boundary effects at <5% collision boundary)

DS-SC2-IDENTITY-01.question: Can toon_id alone serve as the canonical player identity key for sc2egset Phase 02 rating backtesting?
DS-SC2-IDENTITY-01.evidence: toon_id cardinality (K1=2495) = (region,realm,toon_id) cardinality (K2=2495): every toon_id is perfectly region-scoped. Zero cross-region toon_ids detected. Battle.net model confirmed: one toon per (region,realm). BUT: a physical player competing on EU + US + KR receives 3 distinct toon_ids (Serral: 42 toon_ids for LOWER(serral)). Using toon_id alone would split one physical player into N cold-start Elo entities.
DS-SC2-IDENTITY-01.recommendation: REJECT toon_id-alone as canonical key. Use as a component in composite key.
DS-SC2-IDENTITY-01.justification: Invariant I2 requires canonical identity = physical player. toon_id is server-scoped. Multi-server esport players systematically split under toon_id.

DS-SC2-IDENTITY-02.question: Can LOWER(nickname) alone serve as the canonical player identity key for sc2egset?
DS-SC2-IDENTITY-02.evidence: Within-region collision rate: 451/1473 = 30.6% of (nickname,region) pairs have >1 toon_id. Exceeds 5% threshold (Christen 2012 Ch. 5). Top collision: 'showtime' (Europe) has 17 distinct toon_ids. LOWER() merges 61 case variants (K_cs=1106 -> K3=1045), potentially over-merging distinct players with same-cased handles.
DS-SC2-IDENTITY-02.recommendation: REJECT LOWER(nickname)-alone as canonical key. Within-region collision rate far exceeds acceptable threshold.
DS-SC2-IDENTITY-02.justification: Collision rate >30% renders nickname-alone ambiguous. Christen 2012 threshold 5% is violated by 6x.
DS-SC2-IDENTITY-02.routed_to: Phase 02 -- blocking strategy design (must add temporal or region disambiguation)

DS-SC2-IDENTITY-03.question: Do temporal windows of cross-region nickname pairs support a server-switch (disjoint) or concurrent-play (overlap) interpretation?
DS-SC2-IDENTITY-03.evidence: Total cross-nickname toon_id pairs: 16085. Class A (overlap >=1 day): 294 (1.8%). Class B (disjoint): 15474 (96.2%). Class C (degenerate, <2 games): 317 (2.0%). Majority of pairs are B_disjoint, consistent with sequential tournament participation on different servers (not simultaneous multi-account play). Class A overlap pairs indicate concurrent esport tournament presence across servers -- most plausibly the same physical player competing in multiple regional leagues simultaneously.
DS-SC2-IDENTITY-03.recommendation: Treat Class A (overlap) pairs as SAME physical player (merge). Class B (disjoint) as ambiguous -- defer to Phase 02 classifier. Class C insufficient evidence.
DS-SC2-IDENTITY-03.justification: Fellegi & Sunter (1969) agreement pattern. Tournament-day granularity (1-day threshold) is the minimum distinguishable temporal unit in SC2EGSet.

DS-SC2-IDENTITY-04.question: Does the Unknown region cluster (~12.83%) require special handling in identity resolution?
DS-SC2-IDENTITY-04.evidence: Unknown region: 12.83% of replay_players_raw rows (5,750 rows). Unknown toon_ids may not match any region-qualified key. Realm also shows 'Unknown' at same rate -- likely the same rows. These predate full metadata capture in early tournament data (2016-2017 era). Cross-region audit shows all toon_ids in exactly 1 (region,realm) including Unknown -- so 'Unknown' is itself treated as a region by Battle.net metadata extractor.
DS-SC2-IDENTITY-04.recommendation: Treat Unknown as a valid region value for identity key scoping. Do NOT merge Unknown-region toon_ids with same-named known-region toon_ids without temporal evidence.
DS-SC2-IDENTITY-04.justification: Arbitrary merging of Unknown into known-region buckets would inflate cross-region nickname collisions. Temporal evidence (Class A) is the only safe merge signal.
DS-SC2-IDENTITY-04.routed_to: Phase 02 -- Unknown region handling in rating history grouping

DS-SC2-IDENTITY-05.question: What composite key strategy should Phase 02 adopt as the canonical player identity for sc2egset?
DS-SC2-IDENTITY-05.evidence: K1(toon_id)=2495 -- over-splits multi-server players. K3(LOWER(nick))=1045 -- over-merges within-region collisions. K4(LOWER(nick),region)=1473 -- still over-merges (collision rate 30.6%). K5(LOWER(nick),region,realm)=1487 -- granular but unknown-realm noise. Temporal Class A pairs: 294 safe cross-server merges. The data supports: toon_id as the granular unit + LOWER(nickname) as the merge signal, mediated by temporal overlap classification. Class A (overlap) pairs are strong merge candidates; Class B (disjoint) are weak merge candidates (require Phase 02 classifier adjudication).
DS-SC2-IDENTITY-05.recommendation: Phase 02 canonical identity: use toon_id as granular entity; apply LOWER(nickname)-based merging only for Class A temporal overlap pairs. Class B pairs: use separate Elo entities (conservative). Document as Phase 02 decision gate -- requires empirical Elo cold-start sensitivity analysis to validate merge strategy impact.
DS-SC2-IDENTITY-05.justification: Synthesizes I2 (canonical nickname), Fellegi-Sunter classification, and Christen 2012 blocking principles. Conservative strategy minimizes false-merge risk.
DS-SC2-IDENTITY-05.routed_to: Phase 02 -- canonical player_identity_canonical VIEW design + Elo cold-start sensitivity analysis

sql_queries.single_key_census:
SELECT
    COUNT(DISTINCT toon_id)                             AS k1_toon_id,
    COUNT(DISTINCT (region, realm, toon_id))            AS k2_region_realm_toon,
    COUNT(DISTINCT LOWER(nickname))                     AS k3_lower_nick,
    COUNT(DISTINCT (LOWER(nickname), region))           AS k4_lower_nick_region,
    COUNT(DISTINCT (LOWER(nickname), region, realm))    AS k5_lower_nick_region_realm,
    COUNT(DISTINCT nickname)                            AS k_nick_casesensitive
FROM replay_players_raw
sql_queries.toon_id_cross_region_audit:
SELECT
    n_regions,
    n_server_tuples,
    COUNT(*) AS n_toon_ids
FROM (
    SELECT
        toon_id,
        COUNT(DISTINCT region)           AS n_regions,
        COUNT(DISTINCT (region, realm))  AS n_server_tuples
    FROM replay_players_raw
    GROUP BY toon_id
)
GROUP BY n_regions, n_server_tuples
ORDER BY n_regions DESC, n_server_tuples DESC
sql_queries.nickname_cross_region_audit:
SELECT
    LOWER(nickname)                                     AS lower_nick,
    COUNT(DISTINCT toon_id)                             AS n_toon_ids,
    COUNT(DISTINCT region)                              AS n_regions,
    COUNT(DISTINCT (region, realm))                     AS n_server_tuples,
    STRING_AGG(DISTINCT region, ',' ORDER BY region)   AS regions
FROM replay_players_raw
GROUP BY LOWER(nickname)
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT region) > 1
ORDER BY n_toon_ids DESC
sql_queries.temporal_overlap_classification:
WITH player_times AS (
    SELECT
        LOWER(nickname)                     AS lower_nick,
        toon_id,
        region,
        TRY_CAST(details_timeUTC AS TIMESTAMP) AS match_time
    FROM matches_flat
    WHERE nickname IS NOT NULL
),
toon_windows AS (
    SELECT
        lower_nick,
        toon_id,
        region,
        MIN(match_time) AS first_game,
        MAX(match_time) AS last_game,
        COUNT(*)        AS n_games
    FROM player_times
    GROUP BY lower_nick, toon_id, region
),
cross_nick_pairs AS (
    SELECT
        a.lower_nick,
        a.toon_id AS toon_a,
        a.region  AS region_a,
        b.toon_id AS toon_b,
        b.region  AS region_b,
        a.first_game AS first_a, a.last_game AS last_a, a.n_games AS ng_a,
        b.first_game AS first_b, b.last_game AS last_b, b.n_games AS ng_b,
        CASE
            WHEN a.n_games = 1 OR b.n_games = 1
                THEN 'C_degenerate'
            WHEN GREATEST(a.first_game, b.first_game)
                 <= LEAST(a.last_game, b.last_game) - INTERVAL '1 day'
                THEN 'A_overlap'
            ELSE 'B_disjoint'
        END AS temporal_class
    FROM toon_windows a
    JOIN toon_windows b
      ON a.lower_nick = b.lower_nick AND a.toon_id < b.toon_id
)
SELECT temporal_class, COUNT(*) AS n_pairs
FROM cross_nick_pairs
GROUP BY temporal_class
ORDER BY temporal_class
sql_queries.within_region_handle_collision:
SELECT
    LOWER(nickname)  AS lower_nick,
    region,
    COUNT(DISTINCT toon_id) AS n_toon_ids
FROM replay_players_raw
GROUP BY LOWER(nickname), region
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT toon_id) > 1
ORDER BY n_toon_ids DESC
sql_queries.userid_refutation:
SELECT
    COUNT(DISTINCT userID)  AS cardinality,
    MIN(userID)             AS min_userid,
    MAX(userID)             AS max_userid,
    COUNT(DISTINCT userID) <= 16 AS is_slot_index
FROM replay_players_raw
sql_queries.region_realm_sanity:
SELECT
    region,
    COUNT(*) AS n_rows,
    ROUND(COUNT(*) * 100.0 / SUM(COUNT(*)) OVER (), 2) AS pct
FROM replay_players_raw
GROUP BY region
ORDER BY n_rows DESC
sql_queries.robustness_crosscheck:
SELECT
    'matches_flat' AS source,
    COUNT(DISTINCT toon_id)                             AS k1,
    COUNT(DISTINCT (region, realm, toon_id))            AS k2,
    COUNT(DISTINCT LOWER(nickname))                     AS k3,
    COUNT(DISTINCT (LOWER(nickname), region))           AS k4,
    COUNT(DISTINCT (LOWER(nickname), region, realm))    AS k5,
    COUNT(*)                                            AS n_rows
FROM matches_flat
UNION ALL
SELECT
    'matches_flat_clean' AS source,
    COUNT(DISTINCT toon_id)                             AS k1,
    COUNT(DISTINCT (region, realm, toon_id))            AS k2,
    COUNT(DISTINCT LOWER(nickname))                     AS k3,
    COUNT(DISTINCT (LOWER(nickname), region))           AS k4,
    COUNT(DISTINCT (LOWER(nickname), region, realm))    AS k5,
    COUNT(*)                                            AS n_rows
FROM matches_flat_clean

literature: Fellegi, I. P. & Sunter, A. B. (1969). A theory for record linkage. JASA 64(328).
Christen, P. (2012). Data Matching: Concepts and Techniques for Record Linkage, Entity Resolution, and Duplicate Detection. Springer. Ch. 5.
